task_objective: Stop stable releases publishing with an empty body and a bare tag title; author notes in-repo with a generated-notes fallback, and backfill v0.4.0 and v0.4.1.
files_modified: DEVELOPMENT.md (stable release process steps renumbered, notes step added)
design_decisions: release.yml passed neither name nor body to softprops/action-gh-release@v3, so GitHub defaulted to the tag name and an empty body for every stable release to date.
Notes are authored at docs/releases/vX.Y.Z.md with the H1 as the release title and the remainder as the body, so notes are reviewed with the change that motivates them.

A single publish step covers both paths: the action treats empty name and body_path as unset, so the resolver emits empty strings plus generate=true when no file exists. Missing notes degrade to generated notes rather than blocking the release.
Resolution lives in a stdlib-only Python helper rather than inline YAML shell so it is unit-testable without running the workflow, matching version_guard.py and set_release_version.py.
Tag shape is validated against ^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+$ before being used to build a path, so a crafted tag cannot escape docs/releases/.
results: ruff check, ruff format, ty check pass; full suite 860 passed (up from 848) at 88.69% coverage. Backfilled notes published to the existing v0.4.0 and v0.4.1 GitHub releases with gh release edit; tags and assets untouched.
